reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 3 cfs @ Fort Collins , 2 c fs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir Runoff is in the rear view mirror on the Poudre, which means that the bugs are out in great numbers and the fish are eating as many as they can. The summertime heat has brought out a ton of different bugs meaning the fish have had a surplus of food within the river system. A number of different mayfly species have been making there way through the canyon like PMD's and Green Drakes which have been coming off during some of the more humid parts of the day. As well as these mayflies we have also been seeing Golden Stoneflies and Yellow Sallies hatching consistently throughout the day. In the evening the caddis have been coming off thick which has made for some great evening dry fly fishing. Fishing Caddis, Yellow Sally and PMD dries is great way to find success during the early and late parts of the day, with larger Green Drake and Stonefly patterns being effective during the middle of the day. Dry dropper's have also been producing well with larger Stonefly or terrestrial dries above a small Caddis, PMD or Yellow Sally nymph. If you have any questions or are interested in booking a guided trip on the Poudre, feel free to give either shop a call. We'll do our best to get you set up for a great day on the water. Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year. Or, click the images below for a full list of our current recommended fly patterns.
